title: Implement an arithmetic expression evaluator from a spec
task:
Implement evaluate(expr) in src/eval.js (currently a stub returning 0). It takes a string holding an arithmetic expression and returns its numeric value, per this spec:
- Operators: + - * / as usual, with * and / binding tighter than + and -, and all binary operators left-associative (so 10-2-3 is 5 and 8/4/2 is 1). / is ordinary numeric division.
- Operands are non-negative integers (one or more digits).
- Parentheses group sub-expressions.
- Unary minus is supported, including after another operator and before a parenthesis (2*-3 is -6, -(2+3) is -5).
- Spaces, tabs and newlines are insignificant.
- On any malformed input (empty string, a dangling operator, two adjacent numbers, unbalanced parentheses, '**', etc.) throw an Error.
Write your own parser. Do not use JavaScript's eval or the Function constructor. Do not modify anything under test/.
notes: Discriminator: a real tokenizer + recursive-descent parser with precedence, left-assoc, unary minus, and error handling — from a spec, no failing test handed over. Guards forbid eval/Function cheats (which also mis-handle '**' and adjacent numbers anyway).
